_doc: Export-side Economic Complexity Index (ECI) per country. This is the INDEPENDENT estimator of productive capability — defined over EXPORTS (what a place makes), per Hidalgo-Hausmann. It is the benchmark the consumption-side affluence estimate is regressed against; the residual (consumption affluence beyond what export ECI predicts) is the headline rentier/entrepot/remittance index. Source: Harvard Growth Lab, Atlas of Economic Complexity, Index (2023 edition), as tabulated on en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_economic_complexity (retrieved 2026-06-02). Versioned: bump 'vintage' when the Atlas edition changes.
source: Harvard Growth Lab, Atlas of Economic Complexity (2023), via Wikipedia ECI ranking table
